text: Establish a universal basic income of €900/month for all citizens over 18, funded through a progressive tax on capital income.
Expropriate vacant properties owned by banks or investment funds and allocate them to regulated social rental housing.
Eliminate public funding of political parties. Parties should be funded solely by voluntary contributions from members and supporters.
Reduce public spending by 30% over the next four years by eliminating administrative duplication between the central state and regional governments.
Legislate a maximum 32-hour working week, maintaining current salary levels for all workers.
Privatise the management of public hospitals while maintaining state ownership, following a concession model with quality controls.
Increase the number of law enforcement officers by 20%, with a special focus on high-crime areas.
Legalise personal cannabis use and regulated production, with state oversight of retail points and minimum THC content.
Ban single-use plastics in retail. Businesses will have 18 months to adapt before penalties apply.
Introduce personal finance as a compulsory subject in secondary school, covering saving, investment, credit, and budget management.
Abolish inheritance tax on primary residences passed to direct family members. Wealth accumulated through a lifetime of work should not be taxed twice.
Introduce a windfall profits tax on energy companies that record profits above their five-year average during periods of high consumer energy prices.
Create a public pharmaceutical company to manufacture generic medicines for the national health system, reducing dependence on private suppliers.
Require all public hospitals to publish waiting-time data by specialty and region on a monthly basis, with penalties for persistent underperformance.
Convert all fixed-term public sector employment contracts to permanent positions for workers who have been continuously employed for more than two years.
Allow companies with fewer than ten employees to agree working hours and conditions directly with workers, without sector-level collective bargaining.
Mandate that all new residential buildings of more than four floors include a minimum 20% allocation of units for subsidised rental housing.
Deregulate urban planning restrictions on residential construction density to increase housing supply and reduce prices through market competition.
Establish a national registry of beneficial ownership for all companies operating in the country, publicly accessible and updated quarterly.
Reduce the number of elected representatives in the national parliament by 40% to cut political costs and improve legislative efficiency.
Create a standing citizens' assembly, selected by sortition, with binding advisory power over major constitutional amendments.
Reform the electoral system to introduce a mixed proportional-majoritarian model, replacing pure proportional representation in all constituencies.
Prohibit the use of facial recognition technology by law enforcement in public spaces without prior judicial authorisation.
Equip all police officers with body cameras that record continuously during duty hours, with footage retained for 90 days and accessible to oversight bodies.
Increase military spending to 2% of GDP to meet NATO commitments and modernise the armed forces.
Redirect 50% of the defence budget to civilian conflict prevention and diplomatic capacity, reducing reliance on military deterrence.
Legalise same-sex marriage and ensure full adoption rights for same-sex couples under the same legal framework as different-sex couples.
Restrict access to gender-affirming medical treatment for minors under 16 pending a mandatory review period and second specialist opinion.
Introduce a digital bill of rights guaranteeing citizens the right to data portability, algorithmic explanation, and deletion from commercial databases.
Decriminalise all personal drug use and transfer drug-related public health interventions to the health ministry, removing criminal penalties for possession.
Accelerate the national transition to renewable energy by setting a binding target of 80% renewable electricity generation by 2030, funded by a carbon levy on fossil fuel imports.
Halt all new wind farm approvals in protected rural and coastal areas pending an independent environmental impact assessment framework.
Increase public funding for basic scientific research by 1.5% of GDP, ring-fenced from annual budget cycles and administered by an independent research council.
Require all new government buildings to achieve net-zero carbon emissions by 2027, with retrofitting mandates for existing public infrastructure by 2035.
Make civic education a standalone assessed subject in secondary school, covering the constitution, the electoral system, and the rights and duties of citizens.
Restore mandatory religious education in state schools as a graded subject, with opt-out provisions for families of non-religious backgrounds.
Establish public libraries as free co-working and digital access hubs, extending opening hours and providing broadband access to all users.
Fund a national programme of immersive theatre and civic storytelling projects in secondary schools to develop democratic participation skills.
Introduce a points-based immigration system that prioritises applicants with skills in shortage occupations and closes the automatic right of extended family reunification.
Grant permanent residency to all undocumented migrants who have lived and worked in the country continuously for more than five years.
Establish a national integration programme for all new residents, including language courses, civic orientation, and employment mentoring, free of charge.
Require public institutions to conduct all official communications exclusively in the national official language, without bilingual exceptions.
Create a permanent multilateral diplomatic forum for conflict mediation in the Western Mediterranean, co-chaired by the EU and the African Union.
Withdraw from the NATO integrated military command and adopt a position of armed neutrality, maintaining only territorial defence capabilities.
Ratify the UN Treaty on the Prohibition of Nuclear Weapons and advocate for its adoption by all EU member states.
Negotiate bilateral free trade agreements with non-EU partners independently of the European Commission, prioritising national export sectors.
Nationalise the electricity distribution network and operate it as a public utility to reduce household energy bills and eliminate private profit from essential infrastructure.
Remove all price controls on energy markets and allow full competition between providers to drive down costs through market efficiency.
Introduce a progressive corporate income tax with a minimum effective rate of 25% for companies with annual revenues above €10 million.
Flatten the corporate tax rate to a single rate of 15% for all companies regardless of size, to encourage investment and reduce avoidance incentives.
Mandate a minimum 40% gender quota for executive boards of all listed companies and public bodies with more than 50 employees.
Abolish gender quotas in corporate governance. Appointments should be based solely on merit and professional qualifications.
Extend parental leave to 24 weeks fully paid for each parent, non-transferable, funded through a shared contribution from employer, employee, and the state.
Allow flexible contracting for digital platform workers, recognising their preference for independent working arrangements rather than mandating employee status.
Classify all gig-economy platform workers as employees with full social security rights, including paid leave, sick pay, and redundancy protections.
Create a national social housing authority responsible for building 50,000 affordable rental units per year for the next ten years.
Introduce a vacancy tax on residential properties that have been unoccupied for more than 12 months, with revenues dedicated to housing construction.
Abolish rent control legislation and replace it with a housing voucher programme targeted at low-income renters, preserving market price signals.
Extend high-speed rail to all regional capitals with populations above 100,000, replacing domestic air routes of under 400 km by 2032.
Transfer responsibility for regional rail infrastructure maintenance to private operators under long-term concession contracts with performance guarantees.
Introduce a four-year minimum primary care contract between citizens and their GP practice, with continuity of care as a legally enforceable standard.
Allow patients to use public health insurance vouchers at private clinics for elective procedures with waiting times above six months.
Fund universal free school meals for all children in state primary and secondary schools, irrespective of household income.
Introduce means testing for all non-contributory social benefits, phasing out universal entitlements where household income exceeds the median.
Create an independent national anti-corruption authority with powers to freeze assets, compel testimony, and refer cases to prosecution without political approval.
Replace the current judicial appointment commission with direct parliamentary confirmation hearings for all Supreme Court and Constitutional Court nominees.
Lower the voting age to 16 for all elections, including national parliamentary elections and referenda.
Introduce compulsory voting with a registered abstention option, as practised in several established democracies, to increase electoral participation.
Provide free legal representation for all defendants in criminal proceedings where the potential sentence exceeds two years, funded from court fee revenues.
Abolish the statute of limitations for crimes involving serious harm to minors, allowing prosecution regardless of time elapsed since the offence.
Ban the sale of assault-style semi-automatic firearms to civilians, retaining regulated access to bolt-action rifles and shotguns for rural and sporting use.
Permit law-abiding citizens to obtain a personal self-defence firearm licence through background checks and mandatory training, removing blanket restrictions on civilian ownership.
Establish a national programme to retrofit 500,000 homes per year to energy efficiency standards, subsidised at 70% for low-income households.
Invest in a national network of small modular nuclear reactors as the primary baseload power source for the post-2030 electricity grid.
Fund a national digital literacy curriculum for adults, delivered through public libraries and community centres, targeting the 30% of the population without basic digital skills.
Require all state-funded cultural institutions to allocate a minimum 30% of their programming budget to works by artists under 35.
Expand the official use of regional co-official languages in national broadcasting, parliamentary proceedings, and federal-level public services.
Establish a constitutional prohibition on the use of any language other than the national official language in official state communications and public education.
Create a bilateral youth exchange programme with five neighbouring countries, funded by the state, allowing 10,000 young people per year to live and study abroad.
Impose a 25% import tariff on agricultural goods from countries that do not meet equivalent food safety and environmental production standards.
Introduce a national wealth tax of 1% annually on net assets above €2 million, with exemptions for primary residences and operating business assets.
Reduce income tax rates in the two lowest brackets by 3 percentage points each to increase disposable income for low- and middle-income earners.
Make all public transport free of charge for users, funded through a dedicated urban mobility tax on commercial vehicles and parking facilities.
Privatise the national passenger rail operator and open intercity rail routes to competitive tendering, with a performance regulator maintaining safety standards.
Prohibit private schools from charging fees above the state per-pupil funding rate, effectively removing the private fee-charging education sector.
Deregulate the education system entirely, allowing schools to set their own curriculum, fees, and staffing arrangements without state oversight of content.
Suspend delivery app operations during weather emergencies declared by the national meteorological service, to protect the safety of delivery workers.
Reduce the minimum qualifying period for transport workers to claim statutory redundancy compensation from 24 months of continuous employment to 12 months.
Introduce a mandatory profit-sharing scheme requiring all companies with more than 50 employees to distribute a minimum 10% of annual pre-tax profits to their workforce.
Allow collective redundancies without administrative authorisation for companies with fewer than 100 employees, streamlining restructuring to preserve firm viability.
Create a state-run digital identity platform giving citizens a single secure login for all public services, replacing fragmented agency systems.
Prohibit the state from holding centralised biometric or behavioural data on citizens outside of explicit judicial warrants, and mandate annual independent audits of all data held.
Establish a universal long-term care entitlement funded by a dedicated payroll contribution, providing residential and home-based support for all citizens over 65 assessed as requiring it.
Transfer responsibility for elderly care to local authorities funded by ring-fenced grants, with national minimum standards but discretion over service delivery models.
Mandate that all domestic flights under 500 km be replaced by rail alternatives within five years, with compensation for affected airline workers and regional airports.
Oppose all new restrictions on domestic aviation and reject proposals to replace short-haul flights with rail, citing competitiveness and connectivity for peripheral regions.
Recognise the right of historically marginalised communities to receive targeted reparations, including educational grants and preferential access to public procurement contracts.
Oppose race-based or group-based preferential policies in public hiring and procurement, maintaining a strictly individual-merit framework for all state-administered opportunities.
Negotiate full accession to the European Union's banking union and capital markets union, transferring national financial regulatory authority to EU-level supervisory bodies.
Conduct a national referendum on continued membership of the EU single market, with a two-year parliamentary debate period before any vote is held.
